**Clinical Reasoning:** The retrieved evidence discusses microRNAs (miRNAs) extensively in contexts related to osteoporosis and prostate cancer biomarkers, describing them as non-coding regulatory RNAs involved in gene regulation. There is no mention of PCAT6 being classified as a microRNA; instead, PCAT6 refers to a long non-coding RNA (lncRNA) implicated in prostate cancer pathogenesis—distinct from microRNAs. Therefore, PCAT6 is not a microRNA.
